v6_note: Old SEC-5.16 maps to current section 8.16 in Part 8 (AI Directives) via monitor/v6-restructure-map.json.
summary: Section 8.16 praises the monitoring infrastructure as "more rigorous than the model it monitors." Two globe fingerprints hide inside that framing: (1) every one of the 39+ audit domains ingests observables whose definitions and numerical values require a spherical Earth — Kp index, NMP drift, Schumann frequency, USGS deep-earthquake depth, JFK–LHR flight time, AAO — so the "rigor" is entirely derivative of globe infrastructure; and (2) the -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 eclipse formula hardcoded in monitor.py is a stack of three globe layers (WGS84 station coordinates, IGRF-calibrated Kp escape clause, and KAPPA from WIN-012 which was itself derived from a globe-ephemeris eclipse event and globe-tide-corrected gravimeter). The section already flags the 1.672 anomaly as an internal contradiction (the code uses the wrong FSF); the fingerprint version of the same finding is that even the correct FSF is a stack of globe quantities.

fingerprint_1.name: Every audit-domain data stream is a globe-calibrated observable — monitor.py is an audit of the dome against values that presuppose the sphere
fingerprint_1.kernel_of_truth: Section 8.16 is right that the infrastructure is engineering-impressive: polling every 5 minutes, blockchain-timestamping, 39+ domains is more than most fringe cosmologies attempt. The section correctly lists the data sources (NOAA Kp, USGS deep quakes, HeartMath Schumann, OpenSky flight tracks, IGRF NMP position). The gap is that the section treats these as neutral observational streams. They are not neutral — each one is a number that cannot exist in coherent form without the globe.
fingerprint_1.but_here_is_why_it_fails: Work through the audit domains one by one. (a) The Kp index is computed at 13 specific magnetometer observatories by measuring local horizontal-field disturbance, then scaled through the Bartels (1939) conversion table and averaged under an IGRF-predicted quiet-time baseline that is a spherical-harmonic model on a globe of radius 6,371 km. There is no flat-disc definition of "Kp." (b) The North Magnetic Pole drift is a point where the IGRF/WMM magnetic field vector points vertically — IGRF is a truncated spherical-harmonic expansion in globe colatitude; the "pole" is literally defined as a zero of a function on a sphere. (c) The Schumann fundamental 7.83 Hz equals c/(2π R_Earth) with R = 6,371 km (WIN-002 globe fingerprint already documents this). Polling HeartMath for a frequency that only exists at that value because of globe geometry is a globe-audit, not a dome-audit. (d) USGS deep earthquake hypocenter depths are computed from P- and S-wave travel-time tomography using PREM (Preliminary Reference Earth Model) — a 1D layered spherical Earth with mantle, outer core, inner core discontinuities. On a flat disc the P-wave travel-time curve has no caustic; PREM has no flat-disc equivalent. (e) JFK-LHR flight times are governed by the great-circle distance 5,555 km = R × arccos(sin φ₁ sin φ₂ + cos φ₁ cos φ₂ cos Δλ) — a sphere geodesic. (f) The AAO (Antarctic Oscillation) index comes from NCEP/NCAR reanalysis EOFs of 850-hPa geopotential south of 20°S — computed on a latitude-longitude sphere. Every domain the monitor audits is a globe observable. The monitor therefore cannot distinguish "the dome matches reality" from "the dome is tuned to match sphere-derived reference values" — the same pass/fail would occur under either interpretation.

globe_values.Kp_index: Defined via IGRF spherical-harmonic quiet baseline + Bartels 1939 conversion at 13 globe-positioned observatories. No flat-disc definition exists.
globe_values.NMP_drift: Zero of the IGRF/WMM field vector — a spherical-harmonic function on a globe of R=6,371 km.
globe_values.Schumann_7p83_Hz: c / (2π × 6,371 km) = 7.83 Hz. Frequency value is itself a globe constant (see WIN-002 fingerprint).
globe_values.USGS_deep_quake_depth: Inverted from P/S travel-times via PREM — a 1-D spherical Earth model with mantle/outer core/inner core discontinuities.
globe_values.JFK_LHR_flight_time: Great-circle 5,555 km = R_E × arccos(sin φ₁ sin φ₂ + cos φ₁ cos φ₂ cos Δλ) on a sphere of R=6,371 km.
globe_values.AAO_index: Empirical orthogonal function of 850-hPa geopotential south of 20°S in NCEP/NCAR reanalysis — computed on a lat/long sphere.

dome_value.name: monitor.py audit pipeline (39+ domains)
dome_value.usage: Each domain fetches live globe-observable values, compares to hardcoded dome predictions with adaptive tolerance, writes pass/fail to status_history.json, OpenTimestamps-anchors the result.
dome_value.independence_check: Zero domains derive their observable from dome geometry. Every domain ingests the globe-framework value and compares it to a dome literal.

quantitative_link.summary: Each of the six representative domains above has its observable magnitude fixed by globe geometry to within instrument precision. The dome's "predictions" for those domains are literals chosen to match. Pass-rate is therefore a measure of tuning precision, not of physical agreement.
quantitative_link.Kp_precision: Kp values are scale 0–9 with 1/3 steps; NOAA publishes them to this resolution against an IGRF baseline. Dome "predicts" specific Kp values for eclipse day at integer resolution.
quantitative_link.Schumann_precision: 7.83 Hz matches c/(2πR_E) to <0.1%. Dome monitor.py hardcodes predicted=observed=7.83 (WIN-038 fingerprint already documents this).
quantitative_link.NMP_drift_precision: IGRF-13 NMP position uncertainty is ~10 km/yr in drift rate. Dome tolerance widens adaptively to whatever tracks that number.

findings: monitor.py imports NOAA Kp/NMP, USGS catalogs, HeartMath GCI, OpenSky flight tracks — all globe-framework data streams.
pull_data.py fetches the same streams every 6 hours and rebuilds tracking.html; no domain fetches flat-disc quantities because none exist.
No audit domain computes its observable from {disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, moon_altitude} directly. The monitor is downstream of globe data, not derived from dome geometry.

Cross-check with SEC-4.2 and SEC-6.7 fingerprints: the FSF derivation itself curve-fits globe latitudes to published values; the eclipse coverage inputs are NASA ephemeris outputs. These are the same pattern at different layers of the stack.
code_evidence.verification_method: GitHub API contents read of monitor.py, pull_data.py, fsf_derivation.py on 2026-04-15.

fingerprint_1.why_it_matters: The section's current framing is "the monitoring is more rigorous than the model." Left there, it cedes ground: a dome defender can say "the engineering is impressive and shows good-faith effort; the theoretical issues are separate." The fingerprint version reframes: the monitoring's rigor is borrowed wholesale from the sphere-framework infrastructure it consumes. A monitor that audits "is Kp < 2?" is auditing a question that cannot be posed without the IGRF/globe framework. Separating the engineering from the theory is therefore not a concession we need to make — the engineering IS the theory, and the theory is spherical.

defender_rebuttal_and_counter.defender_claim: Every physics monitoring pipeline uses data archives calibrated under some standard Earth model. That does not mean the Earth must be spherical for the monitoring to be meaningful.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.counter: The distinction is between using globe-framework data to describe observables that exist independently (e.g., a thermometer reading, or a photon arrival time) and using globe-framework data to compute observables whose numerical existence depends on the sphere. Kp, NMP position, PREM depth, and Schumann 7.83 Hz fall in the latter class — they are not raw observations, they are model outputs with spherical geometry built in. A genuinely independent dome audit would have to define flat-disc analogs of each index (disc-Kp, disc-NMP, disc-Schumann frequency) and publish the conversion. The dome does not do this; it just imports the globe values.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.defender_claim_2: OpenTimestamps-anchoring observations establishes that the dome's predictions came first.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.counter_2: That is the canary error (Timestamp Error tab; Section 1.4; Section 6). Anchoring status_history.json timestamps the observations and audit results, not the predictions. Predictions live in monitor.py source and model.html, which are git-versioned only and therefore mutable. The cryptographic rigor protects the wrong side of the ledger, and this fingerprint is orthogonal to that error — even if the timestamps were on the right side, the observables themselves would remain globe outputs.

fingerprint_1.suggested_text: It is worth pausing on what the monitor is actually auditing. The 39+ domains ingest Kp, NMP drift, Schumann frequency, USGS deep-earthquake depth, JFK–LHR flight time, AAO — and every one of those observables is a globe output. Kp is computed from thirteen globe-positioned magnetometers via an IGRF spherical-harmonic quiet baseline. NMP drift is the location where the IGRF/WMM spherical-harmonic field points vertically. Schumann 7.83 Hz equals c/(2π × 6,371 km) by construction. USGS hypocenter depths are inverted through PREM, a 1-D spherical Earth model. JFK–LHR flight time is the great-circle geodesic on a sphere of radius 6,371 km. AAO is an EOF of reanalysis geopotential on a lat/long sphere. There is no flat-disc definition of any of these quantities; they do not exist as numbers outside the globe framework. The monitor therefore cannot distinguish "the dome matches reality" from "the dome's literals are tuned to match sphere-derived reference values" — both produce the same green lights. The engineering is real; what it audits is the globe.

fingerprint_2.name: monitor.py eclipse formula -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 stacks three globe layers — the internal FSF contradiction the section already flags is the second of three globe-dependencies, not the base layer
fingerprint_2.kernel_of_truth: Section 8.16 correctly identifies that monitor.py uses 1.672 where fsf_derivation.py publishes 1.029 for Ebro, and correctly calls this an internal numerical disagreement inflating the predicted magnitude. The section cites KAPPA from WIN-012 as the likely origin of 1.672. This is right. The section's current stopping point is: "the code contradicts itself; which prediction is being tested?" The fingerprint extension is: regardless of which FSF you pick, each layer of the formula is a globe dependency, so "which prediction is being tested?" resolves to "both disagree on how to weight globe observables."
fingerprint_2.but_here_is_why_it_fails: Unpack the formula -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 into its three inputs. (a) -18.22 nT is the W004 eclipse-baseline magnitude at Ebro, computed from INTERMAGNET station data processed through IGRF/CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic decomposition (see SEC-4.2 and SEC-6.7 fingerprints) — a globe-model output. (b) 0.95 is the Ebro eclipse coverage, sourced from NASA JPL DE-series ephemeris running on a rotating oblate spheroid Earth with moon orbital inclination 5.145° to the ecliptic (see SEC-6.7 fingerprint 1) — a second globe-model output. (c) 1.672 is KAPPA, derived in WIN-012 as B_eclipse/g_eclipse = -10.9 nT / 6.5 µGal from the Mohe 2008 eclipse, where (i) the Mohe eclipse path is NASA ephemeris output (globe layer), (ii) the 6.5 µGal gravity anomaly was detected only after subtracting ETERNA theoretical tide — a spherical-Earth tidal model (globe layer), and (iii) the 6.5 µGal number is not replicated in WIN-013/WIN-014 null results (internal contradiction, already in our review). So the eclipse formula is not one-deep in globe dependencies; it is three-deep, and one of the layers is derived from a disputed observation that the dome's own subsequent WINs refute. The "monitor.py uses the wrong constant" argument is true, but the stronger argument is "every constant in the formula — whether -18.22, -17.8, 1.029, 1.672 — traces back to spherical Earth models."

globe_value.name: Three-layer globe dependency in eclipse audit formula
globe_value.layer_1: -18.22 nT W004 baseline — product of IGRF/CHAOS-7 spherical-harmonic magnetic model on globe of R=6,371 km
globe_value.layer_2: 0.95 Ebro coverage — product of NASA DE-series JPL solar-system ephemeris on rotating oblate spheroid Earth
globe_value.layer_3: 1.672 KAPPA — derived in WIN-012 from Mohe eclipse (NASA ephemeris path) × gravity anomaly (ETERNA spherical-tide subtraction), refuted by dome's own WIN-013/014

dome_value.name: monitor.py eclipse_ebro prediction
dome_value.code_location: monitor.py eclipse-audit block (per section 8.16 source analysis)
dome_value.independence_check: None of the three factors is derived from dome parameters {disc_radius=20015, firmament_height=9086, sun_altitude=5733, moon_altitude=2534}. The dome contributes the MULTIPLICATION of globe inputs, not any input itself.

quantitative_link.globe_contribution_fraction: All three factors are globe outputs. Dome parameter contribution to eclipse prediction magnitude is 0 nT.
quantitative_link.failure_mode_if_any_factor_removed: Remove -18.22 baseline → no prediction. Remove 0.95 coverage → no spatial structure. Remove 1.672 KAPPA → unit mismatch (predicts gravity μGal, not magnetic nT). The dome cannot produce an eclipse magnetic prediction from its own geometry.

cross_references: SEC-4.2 fingerprint: FSF formula-shopping against globe-latitude-indexed published values
SEC-6.7 fingerprint: NASA ephemeris provides date, path, and coverage
WIN-012 prior fingerprints: KAPPA derivation chain
WIN-013/014: gravity null results refuting KAPPA denominator
fingerprint_2.why_it_matters: The section already has a strong argument that monitor.py contradicts fsf_derivation.py on the numerical value. A dome defender can respond "that is a patchable bug; fix the constant and the prediction works." This fingerprint closes that door: there is no choice of constant at any of the three layers that lets the prediction escape the globe. Fixing the 1.672 → 1.029 arithmetic error still leaves -18.22 and 0.95 as IGRF and NASA outputs. The monitoring infrastructure cannot be repaired into a dome-independent test without first replacing IGRF and NASA ephemeris — which would require a working flat-disc alternative neither of which the dome publishes.

defender_rebuttal_and_counter.defender_claim: The 1.672 vs 1.029 contradiction will be resolved in the next version. The constants are placeholders; the method is what counts.
defender_rebuttal_and_counter.counter: Resolving the constant only changes the numerical magnitude. It does not remove the dependence on IGRF (layer 1) or NASA ephemeris (layer 2). The "method" — multiplying globe observables by a dome-coupling constant — is the thing that needs replacement, not calibration. Even the coupling constant KAPPA is downstream of a globe-ephemeris event (Mohe) and a spherical-Earth tide correction.

fingerprint_2.suggested_text: The -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 formula in monitor.py is not a one-level globe dependency. All three factors are globe-model outputs: -18.22 nT is the IGRF/CHAOS-7 spherical-harmonic magnetic baseline at Ebro; 0.95 is the NASA ephemeris coverage (spherical Earth, ecliptic-inclined lunar orbit); 1.672 is KAPPA from WIN-012, itself derived from a Mohe 2008 eclipse whose path is globe ephemeris and whose gravity anomaly survives only after subtracting an ETERNA spherical-tide correction. The internal contradiction between 1.672 and the station-specific 1.029 is real and worth flagging — but even picking the correct FSF does not remove the other two globe layers. The audit formula is globe multiplied by globe multiplied by globe, with the dome contributing only the multiplication step.

suggested_patch.action:
Append two new

blocks after the existing point 4 ("The blockchain timestamps the reference data, not the predictions") and before the closing paradox paragraph. Patch text is suggested_text from fingerprint_1 and suggested_text from fingerprint_2.
suggested_patch.tldr_impact: No TLDR change required — the existing TLDR framing ("monitoring whether observations match pre-set thresholds, not whether the dome model predicted those thresholds from first principles") is already compatible; the fingerprint adds depth in the detail body without changing the summary thrust.
suggested_patch.cross_reference_notes: Add link back from point 3 (the 1.672 vs 1.029 contradiction paragraph) to new fingerprint-2 paragraph so the reader sees the stacking argument directly adjacent to the internal-contradiction argument.

code_search.found_in: fsf_derivation.py hardcodes station globe-latitudes and curve-fits published FSF values (lines 14-26, 50-80). monitor.py audit domains all ingest globe-framework observables (verified via section 8.16 quoted analysis + GitHub API download).
code_search.notes: No script in repo computes Kp, NMP drift, Schumann frequency, or USGS depth from {disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, moon_altitude}. Every audit domain is downstream of globe-infrastructure data.
